notes: opencode is asked (via a seeded AGENTS.md rule) to emit a hidden HTML-comment progress marker `<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} -->` in its own assistant text and increment completed_rounds across a 3-phase task, settling to ready at the end. The OBSERVABLE LIFECYCLE arc (ready→working→ready across the multi-turn task, final state ready, no child sessions) is asserted as phases below. The load-bearing observable this scenario exists for — task_estimate {total_rounds,completed_rounds,updated_at} present on the session metrics while working, task_completion_eta burning down as completed_rounds climbs, and BOTH fields suppressed once the session leaves working — is now PRODUCED for opencode as of commit 1ae457d9: the per-adapter marker scan was promoted to the shared tailer.ScanTaskEstimate and wired into opencode's custom SQLite metrics path (querySessionMetrics sets metrics.TaskEstimate + projects task_completion_eta via session.ForecastTaskCompletion, latest-wins), plus parseTextPart scans the full part text before the 200-rune truncation. The marker trace is plain text in opencode's SQLite part.data and is fully readable. NOT known_failing on the daemon any longer. (task_estimate/task_completion_eta have no phase-DSL assertion form — they are metric fields — so the now-observable signal is documented here; the phase lines assert the lifecycle arc. Whether the recorded ~2-4B local Gemma actually emits/increments the marker is an agent=partial question a live recording settles, not a daemon gap.)

text: opencode ses_… session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth)
First prompt (plan + phase 1, emit marker total_rounds=3 completed_rounds=0→1) drives ready → working
Phase-1 turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session
Second prompt (phase 2, marker completed_rounds=2) drives the same session ready → working
Phase-2 turn completes back to ready on the same session
Final prompt (phase 3, marker completed_rounds=3 == total_rounds) drives the same session ready → working
Final phase completes; session settles to ready (final state ready) after a ≥10s trailing idle; no subagents — at ready the task_estimate/task_completion_eta fields are absent (suppressed-by-state)
